g.text: Three protected promotion branches, sequential policy gates, Azure OIDC, isolated remote state, and gated apply
One azure/* work branch targets one protected environment branch
Sequential and fail-fast: context -> format -> validate -> security -> plan -> OPA
Hub-owned backend with isolated containers and keys
Closed PR event, explicit gate, fresh plan, environment-scoped execution
Federated Entra identity spans the three subscriptions and hub state
Permissions, concurrency, notifications, reusable modules, and hardening backlog
Opens PR into the intended target
No direct push to protected branches
Unsupported target branch fails context mapping
No Azure login or remote state
main also tests prod-dr plan JSON
OPA is the current hard policy gate
They are NOT reused after merge
Blob locking prevents concurrent state mutation
No Azure login or state mutation
Does not trust stale PR artifact
main: prod complete, then prod-DR complete
Apply failure -> Slack/email with workflow URL
Success leaves desired configuration in remote state
HUB 5b942f88... -> shared networking, DNS, ACR, Front Door, tfstate
PROD a8270be7... -> environments/prod -> Central India primary
PROD same subscription -> environments/prod-dr -> South India DR
Hub provider alias + hub remote state support cross-subscription DNS/peering
Apply uses the same minimum GitHub permissions
PR concurrency cancels stale runs per PR
Apply concurrency serializes each base branch and never cancels
AzureRM environment variables select the mapped subscription
PR stage failure | rejected apply gate | apply failure
No Azure client secret is stored
naming | resource-group | networking | vnet-peering | private-dns | private-endpoint
firewall | ACR | Front Door | App Gateway WAF | AKS | PostgreSQL Flexible
Key Vault | Service Bus | Monitor | AI Foundry | managed identity | policy
Environment roots compose modules; modules do not own backends or branch logic
HARD: context mapping, fmt, validate, Azure login, backend init, plan, OPA, merge, label, approval, apply
PR plans are review evidence; apply always creates a new plan
State keys isolate blast radius; branch and apply concurrency prevent overlap
Pin Checkov instead of installing latest; remove --soft-fail after backlog closure
Apply workflow triggers on every closed PR to target branches, including docs-only PRs
Add plan summary/comment and explicit destructive-change review
Use private/self-hosted runner before tfstate/Key Vault public lockdown
Reduce User Access Administrator scope or split RBAC bootstrap
Add drift detection and scheduled read-only plans
